"Initial Conception as a "Trojan Horse": Seth MacFarlane, a massive Star Trek fan, wanted to create a show with the optimistic, thoughtful tone of TNG. Because the network wanted a comedy, he masked this serious intention with humor in the first season. 

From Spoof to Sincere: By the end of the first season, and especially in seasons 2 and 3 ("New Horizons"), the show moved away from slapstick or crude humor, instead focusing on character-driven drama, social commentary, and high-concept sci-fi."

replies.text:
I had assumed that SAM had a mobile holo-emitter, which was hit on the Miyazaki, after she over-confidently stood in enemy fire. However, apparently she doesn't have one... so none of it makes any sense. The whole idea of the Doctor becoming autonomous, by running his program locally on a mobile emitter, was essential to his character development in VOY. Much like Data in TNG, it raised questions about rights, freedoms, and the question of 'what is life?'. In this show, 'hologram' apparently just means 'incorporeal being'.
It's like the writers heard that Robert Picardo 'played a hologram' on past Trek, and simply didn't give it any thought beyond that.

I have started to watch startrek only after every one started to rip SFA to shreds to see how bad it really is compared to the OG.
And boy ohhh boy, just from the first episode the difference is evident as day from night, when the doc shoots the shapeshifting alien that was about to kill Kirk after hesitating because the alien took his lovers/crush/missed love form,  Kirk doesn't shout or curse at him for hesitating and putting his, i.e Kirks, life in danger, no, Kirk just looks at him and says "im sorry Bones.", just this moment proved to me how much they F up modern startrek...
